parlamentary_course.title: Basics of Parliament Functioning
parlamentary_course.description: Learn about the structure, roles, and legislative processes of the Polish parliament.
parlamentary_course.desc: In this course you will learn how the Polish parliament works and how the legislative process takes place. You will also learn about key institutions and roles in the Polish political system!

modules.title: Separation of Powers
modules.text: The Polish political system is based on the separation of legislative, executive, and judicial powers.

modules.title: Legislative Power
modules.text: In Poland, legislative power is exercised by the Sejm and the Senate, forming a bicameral parliament.

modules.title: Role of Parliament
modules.text: The main task of parliament is law-making, i.e. passing national laws.

modules.title: Sejm and Senate
modules.text: Both chambers work separately but cooperate in the legislative process on each bill.

modules.title: Principle of Representation
modules.text: Members of Parliament and senators represent the entire Nation, not only their voters.

modules.title: Parliamentary Term
modules.text: The term of the Sejm and Senate lasts 4 years and begins on the day of the first sitting.

quiz.question: What is legislative power?
How long is the term of the Sejm and Senate?
Who exercises legislative power in Poland?

l2.title: Structure of the Sejm of the Republic of Poland

modules.title: Number of Deputies
modules.text: The Sejm of the Republic of Poland consists of 460 deputies elected in general elections.

modules.title: Marshal of the Sejm
modules.text: The Marshal of the Sejm presides over sessions, represents the Sejm, and safeguards its rights.

modules.title: Presidium of the Sejm
modules.text: The Presidium of the Sejm consists of the Marshal and Deputy Marshals.

modules.title: Council of Seniors
modules.text: The Council of Seniors is a body ensuring cooperation between parliamentary clubs and groups.

modules.title: Clubs and Groups
modules.text: Deputies may form clubs (at least 15 deputies) or groups (at least 3 deputies).

modules.title: Chancellery of the Sejm
modules.text: The Chancellery of the Sejm provides organizational and technical support for deputies.

quiz.question: How many deputies are in the Sejm of the Republic of Poland?
Who presides over the Sejm sessions?
What is the Council of Seniors?
answers: A body coordinating clubs and groups

l3.title: Structure of the Senate of the Republic of Poland

modules.title: Number of Senators
modules.text: The Senate of the Republic of Poland consists of 100 senators elected in single-member constituencies.

modules.title: House of Reflection
modules.text: The Senate is often called the 'chamber of reflection' on legislation from the Sejm.

modules.title: Marshal of the Senate
modules.text: The Marshal of the Senate represents the chamber and presides over its sessions.

modules.title: Presidium of the Senate
modules.text: The Presidium of the Senate consists of the Marshal and Deputy Marshals.

modules.title: Senate Elections
modules.text: Senators are elected in universal, direct, and secret ballot elections.

modules.title: Single-member districts
modules.text: In Senate elections, one senator is elected from each electoral district.

quiz.question: How many senators are in the Senate of the Republic of Poland?
In what districts are senators elected?
Who presides over Senate sessions?

modules.title: Free Mandate
modules.text: A parliamentarian is not bound by voters' instructions and cannot be recalled.

modules.title: Parliamentary Immunity
modules.text: Immunity protects a parliamentarian from criminal liability without the consent of the chamber.

modules.title: Inviolability
modules.text: A deputy or senator cannot be detained without consent of the chamber unless caught in the act.

modules.title: Rights of a Deputy
modules.text: Parliamentarians have the right to vote and submit interpellations.

modules.title: Duties
modules.text: The main duty is active participation in parliamentary and committee work.

modules.title: Parliamentary Ethics
modules.text: Deputies' conduct is supervised by the Parliamentary Ethics Committee, which may issue reprimands.

quiz.question: What does a free mandate mean?
answers: No obligation to follow voters' instructions

quiz.question: What protects a parliamentarian from arrest?
Who may reprimand a deputy for ethical violations?

modules.title: Role of Committees
modules.text: Committees are groups of deputies or senators appointed to examine specific issues and draft laws.

modules.title: Standing Committees
modules.text: Standing committees operate throughout the entire term (e.g. Foreign Affairs, Finance).

modules.title: Ad hoc Committees
modules.text: Ad hoc committees are created to perform a single specific task.

modules.title: Inquiry Committees
modules.text: The Sejm may appoint an inquiry committee to investigate a specific public issue.

modules.title: Composition of Committees
modules.text: Committee composition reflects the proportional representation of parliamentary clubs.

modules.title: Subcommittees
modules.text: Committees may create smaller working subcommittees for detailed analysis of bills.

quiz.question: Which committee investigates special matters?
What determines committee composition?
Which committees operate throughout the term?

modules.title: Definition
modules.text: Legislative initiative is the right to submit a draft law to the Sejm.

modules.title: Authorized Entities
modules.text: This right belongs to Deputies, the Senate, the President, the Council of Ministers, and citizens.

modules.title: Parliamentary Initiative
modules.text: A bill may be submitted by at least 15 deputies or a parliamentary committee.

modules.title: Citizens' Initiative
modules.text: At least 100,000 citizens with voting rights may submit a bill.

modules.title: Government Initiative
modules.text: Government bills make up the majority of adopted laws.

modules.title: Formal Requirements
modules.text: Each bill must include justification and financial consequences.

quiz.question: How many deputies are needed for a parliamentary bill?
How many signatures are required for a citizens' initiative?
Who can submit a bill to the Sejm?
answers: Deputies, Senate, President, Council of Ministers, and citizens

modules.title: First Reading
modules.text: The first reading involves presenting the bill and takes place in the Sejm or committee.

modules.title: Committee Work
modules.text: After the first reading, the bill goes to committee for detailed analysis.

modules.title: Second Reading
modules.text: The second reading includes committee reports and proposed amendments.

modules.title: Third Reading
modules.text: The third reading is a final vote on the bill including amendments.

modules.title: Majority Vote
modules.text: Most laws are passed by a simple majority in the presence of at least half of deputies.

modules.title: Quorum
modules.text: Quorum is the minimum number of deputies (half, i.e. 230) required for a valid vote.

quiz.question: How many readings does a bill go through?
What is the quorum in the Sejm?
What happens during the second reading?

l8.title: Role of the Senate and the President

modules.title: Submission to the Senate
modules.text: The Marshal of the Sejm sends the adopted bill to the Senate.

modules.title: Senate Timeframe
modules.text: The Senate has 30 days to adopt, amend, or reject a bill.

modules.title: Overriding Senate Veto
modules.text: The Sejm may reject Senate amendments or veto by absolute majority.

modules.title: Role of the President
modules.text: After parliamentary approval, the bill is sent to the President for signature.

modules.title: Presidential Veto
modules.text: The President may veto a bill and return it to the Sejm.

modules.title: Constitutional Tribunal
modules.text: The President may refer a bill to the Constitutional Tribunal before signing.

quiz.question: How many days does the Senate have to review a bill?
To whom does a bill go after passing the Sejm and Senate?
What can the President do with a bill they do not want to sign?

modules.title: Nature of Control
modules.text: The Sejm controls the Council of Ministers and other state administration bodies.

modules.title: Parliamentary Interpellation
modules.text: A deputy may submit an interpellation to a minister on important matters.

modules.title: Parliamentary Questions
modules.text: Questions concern individual or specific issues.

modules.title: Vote of No Confidence
modules.text: The Sejm may pass a vote of no confidence against a minister or government.

modules.title: Constructive Vote of No Confidence
modules.text: Must include a named candidate for Prime Minister.

modules.title: Supreme Audit Office
modules.text: The Supreme Audit Office is subordinate to the Sejm and is the main audit body.

quiz.question: Which chamber has a constitutional control function?
What is a constructive vote of no confidence?
Which body is subordinate to the Sejm and controls the state?

modules.title: Definition
modules.text: The National Assembly is a joint sitting of deputies and senators chaired by the Marshal of the Sejm.

modules.title: Presidential Oath
modules.text: Its main task is to administer the oath of office to the President of Poland.

modules.title: Presidential Address
modules.text: It also listens to official addresses of the President.

modules.title: Determining Incapacity
modules.text: It may declare the President permanently incapable of holding office.

modules.title: Impeachment
modules.text: It may bring the President before the State Tribunal.

modules.title: Qualified Majority
modules.text: Requires a 2/3 majority of the statutory number of members.

quiz.question: Who forms the National Assembly?
Who presides over the National Assembly?
What majority is required to impeach the President?

modules.title: Most Important Act
modules.text: The budget act is the annual financial plan of the state.

modules.title: Government Exclusivity
modules.text: Only the Council of Ministers may submit the draft budget.

modules.title: Role of the Senate
modules.text: The Senate may propose amendments but cannot reject the budget.

modules.title: Presidential Deadline
modules.text: The President must sign the budget within 7 days and cannot veto it.

modules.title: Parliament Dissolution
modules.text: If the budget is not submitted within 4 months, the President may dissolve parliament.

modules.title: Report
modules.text: The government must submit an execution report and the Sejm decides on discharge.

quiz.question: Who has exclusive right to submit the budget bill?
Can the President veto the budget act?
What can the President do if the budget is not submitted within 4 months?

modules.title: Nature of Responsibility
modules.text: Deputies and senators may be held accountable for violating the Constitution or laws.

modules.title: State Tribunal
modules.text: The State Tribunal judges top state officials and parliamentarians.

modules.title: Ban on Business Activity
modules.text: MPs are forbidden from conducting business using state property.

modules.title: Asset Declarations
modules.text: MPs and senators must submit annual public asset declarations.

modules.title: Waiving Immunity
modules.text: A parliamentarian may waive immunity voluntarily.

modules.title: Loss of Mandate
modules.text: A final conviction for an intentional crime results in loss of mandate.

quiz.question: Which body judges MPs for constitutional violations?
What causes automatic loss of mandate?
Can a parliamentarian waive immunity?

questions.question: How long is the term of the Sejm and Senate?
How many MPs sit in the Polish Sejm?
How many senators are in the Polish Senate?
Who can submit a bill to the Sejm?
answers: MPs, Senate, President, Council of Ministers, and citizens

questions.question: How many readings does a bill go through in the Sejm?
Which body tries the highest officials for violating the Constitution?
What is a presidential veto?
answers: The President's refusal to sign a bill
The Senate's rejection of a bill
The Prime Minister's motion to reject a bill

questions.question: What is parliamentary immunity?
answers: Protection of an MP from criminal liability for parliamentary activities
A ban on holding other positions

questions.question: Can an MP waive their own immunity?
What is the Council of Elders (Konwent Seniorów) in the Sejm?
answers: A council of the oldest MPs
A body ensuring cooperation between parliamentary clubs and circles
A historical committee of the Sejm

executive_course.title: The Role and Functioning of the Government
executive_course.description: Understand how the government works, its structures and decision-making processes.
executive_course.desc: In this course, you will learn how the Polish government works, its structures and decision-making processes. You will also get to know the key institutions and roles in the Polish political system!

modules.title: Dualism of the Executive
modules.text: Executive power in Poland is shared by two entities: the President of the Republic of Poland and the Council of Ministers.

modules.title: Council of Ministers
modules.text: The Council of Ministers, i.e. the government, conducts the day-to-day internal and foreign policy of the state.

modules.title: President as Arbiter
modules.text: The President is the supreme representative of the Republic of Poland, ensures compliance with the Constitution and acts as an arbiter.

modules.title: Division of Tasks
modules.text: The government manages administration and finances, while the President is responsible for supreme command of the armed forces and representation.

modules.title: Constitutional Role of the Government
modules.text: The Council of Ministers is responsible for all state policy matters not reserved for other bodies.

modules.title: Coordination
modules.text: The government ensures the implementation of laws by issuing executive acts and directing government administration.

quiz.question: Who creates the dualism of executive power in Poland?
answers: The President and the Council of Ministers

quiz.question: Who conducts the day-to-day internal and foreign policy?
What is the role of the President in the executive power system?
answers: Ensures compliance with the Constitution and acts as arbiter

l2.title: Composition and Structure of the Government

modules.title: Prime Minister
modules.text: The government is headed by the President of the Council of Ministers, commonly known as the Prime Minister.

modules.title: Deputy Prime Ministers
modules.text: The government may include Deputy Presidents of the Council of Ministers (Deputy Prime Ministers).

modules.title: Ministers in Charge of Ministries
modules.text: Ministers head specific branches of administration (e.g. Minister of Health).

modules.title: Ministers without Portfolio
modules.text: These are ministers who do not head a specific ministry but perform tasks assigned by the Prime Minister.

modules.title: Government Committees
modules.text: Government committees operate within the government structure (e.g. the Standing Committee of the Council of Ministers) preparing draft documents.

modules.title: Collegiality of the Government
modules.text: The Council of Ministers takes the most important decisions collectively in the form of resolutions.

quiz.question: Who heads the Council of Ministers?
What is a minister without portfolio?
answers: A minister without a ministry performing tasks assigned by the Prime Minister

quiz.question: What committees operate within the government structure?
answers: Government Committees (e.g. Standing Committee of the Council of Ministers)

modules.title: First Step
modules.text: The President designates the Prime Minister, who proposes the composition of the Council of Ministers.

modules.title: Appointment by the President
modules.text: The President appoints the Prime Minister and ministers within 14 days of the first sitting of the Sejm.

modules.title: Exposé and Vote of Confidence
modules.text: The Prime Minister presents the government's programme (exposé) to the Sejm and requests a vote of confidence.

modules.title: Absolute Majority
modules.text: The Sejm passes a vote of confidence by an absolute majority of votes in the presence of at least half of the MPs.

modules.title: Second Step (Sejm)
modules.text: If the government does not receive a vote of confidence, within 14 days the Sejm elects the Prime Minister and the government by absolute majority.

modules.title: Third Step
modules.text: If the Sejm fails, the President appoints the government again, but then the Sejm requires only a simple majority.

quiz.question: What is the name of the Prime Minister's programmatic speech in the Sejm?
Who elects the government in the second constitutional step?
By what majority does the Sejm pass a vote of confidence for the government?

l4.title: Role and Tasks of the Prime Minister

modules.title: Managing the Work
modules.text: The Prime Minister represents the Council of Ministers and directs its work.

modules.title: Coordination of Ministers
modules.text: The Prime Minister coordinates and supervises the work of all other government ministers.

modules.title: Issuing Regulations
modules.text: The Prime Minister is a one-person body and has the right to issue his/her own regulations.

modules.title: Superior of Civil Servants
modules.text: The Prime Minister is the service superior of all civil service employees.

modules.title: Division of Duties
modules.text: The Prime Minister decides on the scope of duties and competences of ministers.

modules.title: Government Resignation
modules.text: The resignation of the Prime Minister automatically means the resignation of the entire Council of Ministers.

quiz.question: What happens to the government when the Prime Minister resigns?
answers: A new Deputy Prime Minister is elected

quiz.question: Who is the superior of the civil service corps?
answers: Head of the Ministry of Interior

quiz.question: Does the Prime Minister have the right to issue his/her own regulations?
answers: Only with the consent of the Sejm

modules.title: Role of a Ministry
modules.text: A ministry is an office that provides support to the minister implementing state policy.

modules.title: Secretaries of State
modules.text: Deputy ministers (secretaries and undersecretaries of state) assist the minister in managing the department.

modules.title: Director General
modules.text: The Director General ensures the continuity of the ministry's work and manages administrative matters.

modules.title: Central Offices
modules.text: In addition to ministries, central offices operate under ministers (e.g. Statistics Poland).

modules.title: Branches of Administration
modules.text: One minister may head several branches of government administration (e.g. education and science).

modules.title: Regulations
modules.text: Ministers issue regulations based on explicit authorisation contained in statutes.

quiz.question: Who is responsible for the continuity of operations and administrative matters in a ministry?
On what basis can a minister issue a regulation?
Can one minister head several branches of government administration?

modules.title: Government Initiative
modules.text: Ministries prepare assumptions for draft laws and regulations.

modules.title: Public Consultations
modules.text: Government drafts undergo mandatory inter-ministerial and public consultations.

modules.title: Legislative Council
modules.text: The Legislative Council reviews drafts for compliance with the Polish legal system.

modules.title: Standing Committee
modules.text: The Standing Committee of the Council of Ministers resolves disputes between ministers before the government meeting.

modules.title: Government Meeting
modules.text: The government debates drafts at closed meetings and adopts documents.

modules.title: Public Information Bulletin
modules.text: All adopted government drafts and plans are published in the BIP system.

quiz.question: Where does a government draft go before the full Council of Ministers meeting to resolve disputes?
answers: To the Standing Committee of the Council of Ministers

quiz.question: Are Council of Ministers meetings generally open to the public?
Where can one find information about adopted government drafts?

modules.title: Government Representative
modules.text: The Voivode is the representative of the Council of Ministers in each of the 16 voivodeships.

modules.title: Supervision of Local Government
modules.text: The Voivode supervises the legality of resolutions adopted by communes, districts and regional assemblies.

modules.title: Integrated Administration
modules.text: The Voivode manages services responsible for security (e.g. Police, Fire Service in the voivodeship).

modules.title: Appointment of Voivode
modules.text: The Voivode is appointed and dismissed by the Prime Minister at the request of the minister responsible for administration.

modules.title: Local Law Acts
modules.text: The Voivode has the power to issue regulations and local law acts in crisis situations.

modules.title: Crisis Management
modules.text: The Voivode coordinates rescue operations in the voivodeship during natural disasters.

quiz.question: Who is the Voivode?
answers: Representative of the government in the voivodeship

quiz.question: Who appoints the Voivode?
answers: President of the Republic of Poland

quiz.question: What role does the Voivode play during natural disasters?
answers: Manages evacuation of the entire country

modules.title: Task of the Civil Service
modules.text: The civil service corps ensures professional, reliable and apolitical performance of state tasks.

modules.title: Apolitical Nature
modules.text: Civil service officials may not manifest political views or form political parties.

modules.title: Strike Ban
modules.text: Appointed civil service officials may not take actions that disrupt the work of the office (e.g. strikes).

modules.title: Recruitment
modules.text: Recruitment to the civil service is open, competitive and based on competence criteria.

modules.title: Head of the Civil Service
modules.text: The work of the corps is directed by the Head of the Civil Service, who reports directly to the Prime Minister.

modules.title: Continuity of the State
modules.text: Thanks to the civil service, offices operate stably even during a change of the ruling party.

quiz.question: What key characteristic must a civil service official have?
To whom does the Head of the Civil Service report?
Can civil service officials strike?

modules.title: Government Responsibility
modules.text: The Council of Ministers is responsible for ensuring the external and internal security of the state.

modules.title: Defence Ministry
modules.text: The Minister of National Defence directs the army and implements the government's defence policy.

modules.title: Internal Security
modules.text: The Minister of the Interior and Administration supervises the Police, Border Guard and SOP.

modules.title: Government Crisis Team
modules.text: In threat situations, the Government Crisis Management Team meets under the chairmanship of the Prime Minister.

modules.title: Extraordinary States
modules.text: The government may request the President to introduce a state of emergency or martial law.

modules.title: Army Funding
modules.text: The government ensures stable funding for the armed forces through the state budget.

quiz.question: Which minister is responsible for supervising the Police and Border Guard?
answers: Minister of the Interior and Administration

quiz.question: Who chairs the Government Crisis Management Team?
Can the government request the introduction of a state of emergency?

modules.title: Conducting Policy
modules.text: The Council of Ministers exercises general direction in relations with other states.

modules.title: Role of MFA
modules.text: The Minister of Foreign Affairs coordinates the implementation of foreign policy and supervises embassies.

modules.title: International Agreements
modules.text: The government concludes international agreements, which sometimes require ratification by parliament or the President.

modules.title: Representation in the EU
modules.text: The Prime Minister and ministers represent Poland's interests in the European Council and the Council of the EU.

modules.title: Cooperation with the President
modules.text: The government is obliged to cooperate with the President on foreign policy matters.

modules.title: Ambassadors
modules.text: The government proposes the appointment of ambassadors, who are officially appointed by the President of the Republic of Poland.

quiz.question: Who directly coordinates foreign policy and supervises embassies?
Where does the Prime Minister represent Poland at the highest EU level?
Who officially appoints ambassadors?
answers: President of the Republic of Poland

modules.title: Political Accountability
modules.text: The Council of Ministers is politically accountable to the Sejm of the Republic of Poland.

modules.title: Vote of Confidence During Term
modules.text: The Prime Minister may at any time ask the Sejm for a vote of confidence in the government.

modules.title: Vote of No Confidence in a Minister
modules.text: The Sejm may pass a vote of no confidence in an individual minister on the motion of at least 69 MPs.

modules.title: Resignation after Elections
modules.text: The Prime Minister always submits the resignation of the government at the first sitting of the newly elected Sejm.

modules.title: Tribunal of State
modules.text: Members of the government are accountable before the Tribunal of State for violations of law or the Constitution.

modules.title: NIK Control
modules.text: The Supreme Audit Office regularly examines government expenditure and the legality of ministry actions.

quiz.question: When does the Prime Minister have a constitutional obligation to submit the government's resignation?
answers: At the first sitting of the new Sejm

quiz.question: Before whom are members of the government accountable for violating the Constitution?
How many MPs' signatures are needed to submit a vote of no confidence in a minister?

modules.title: Countersignature
modules.text: Most legal acts of the President require the signature (countersignature) of the Prime Minister to take effect.

modules.title: Prerogative Acts
modules.text: Prerogatives are the President's personal powers that do not require the Prime Minister's countersignature (e.g. right of pardon).

modules.title: Cabinet Council
modules.text: The Cabinet Council is the government meeting under the chairmanship of the President. It has no decision-making powers of the government.

modules.title: Purpose of the Council
modules.text: The President convenes the Cabinet Council on matters of particular importance to the state.

modules.title: Supreme Command of the Armed Forces
modules.text: The President is the Supreme Commander of the Armed Forces, but in peacetime exercises this through the Minister of National Defence.

modules.title: Consultations
modules.text: Regular meetings between the Prime Minister and the President are crucial for the stability of state policy.

quiz.question: What is the Prime Minister's signature on the President's act?
Can the Cabinet Council under the President make government resolutions?
answers: No (it has only a consultative character)
Yes, with the consent of the Sejm

quiz.question: Who is the Supreme Commander of the Armed Forces in peacetime?
answers: President through the Minister of National Defence

questions.question: Who forms the dual executive power in Poland?
answers: Prime Minister and Speaker of the Sejm

questions.question: Who conducts the day-to-day internal and foreign policy of the state?
What is a vote of confidence?
answers: A Sejm vote supporting the government's activities
The dismissal of a minister by the Sejm
A motion for the Prime Minister's resignation

questions.question: What does a constructive vote of no confidence involve?
answers: Dismissing the government without naming a new Prime Minister
Dismissing the government while simultaneously electing a new Prime Minister
The President's motion to dismiss the government

questions.question: What is a countersignature (kontrasygnata)?
answers: The Prime Minister's or minister's signature on a presidential act
The President's signature on Sejm bills

questions.question: Who is the supreme commander of the Armed Forces in peacetime?
answers: President through the Ministry of National Defence

questions.question: What is the Cabinet Council (Rada Gabinetowa)?
answers: A government session chaired by the President without the government's decision-making powers
A permanent body coordinating ministerial work
A parliamentary commission on government affairs

questions.question: Who appoints and dismisses ministers?
answers: The Sejm on the Prime Minister's motion
The President on the motion of the Prime Minister

questions.question: What role does the Prime Minister play?
answers: Leads the Council of Ministers and coordinates the government's work
Represents Poland abroad as head of state
Chairs the sessions of the Sejm

questions.question: What are presidential prerogatives?
answers: Presidential powers requiring the Prime Minister's countersignature
Personal presidential powers not requiring a countersignature
Special Sejm powers over the President

court_course.title: The Judicial System in Poland
court_course.description: Learn about the structure and functioning of the Polish judicial system.
court_course.desc: In this course, you will learn how the Polish judicial system works, its structures and decision-making processes. You will also get to know the key institutions and roles in the Polish legal system!

modules.title: The Third Power
modules.text: Judicial power is independent from legislative and executive power.

modules.title: Courts and Tribunals
modules.text: Judicial power in Poland is exercised by courts and independent tribunals.

modules.title: Judicial Independence
modules.text: Judges are independent, meaning they are subject only to the Constitution and statutes.

modules.title: Appeal and Instances
modules.text: Court proceedings in Poland are at least two-instance (it is possible to appeal).

modules.title: Issuing Judgments
modules.text: Courts issue judgments in the name of the Republic of Poland.

modules.title: Public Hearings
modules.text: Court hearings are public unless the law allows exclusion of the public for reasons of morality or security.

quiz.question: To whom are independent judges subject?
How many instances are court proceedings in Poland?
Are court hearings in Poland public?
answers: Yes, unless the law provides otherwise

modules.title: District Courts
modules.text: District courts are the lowest level and handle most minor civil and criminal cases.

modules.title: Regional Courts
modules.text: Regional courts hear more serious cases as the first instance and appeals from district courts.

modules.title: Courts of Appeal
modules.text: Courts of Appeal deal exclusively with appeals against judgments of regional courts.

modules.title: Court Divisions
modules.text: Courts are divided into divisions: civil, criminal, family, labour, and commercial.

modules.title: Lay Judges
modules.text: In some cases, citizens – lay judges – sit alongside professional judges.

modules.title: Territorial Jurisdiction
modules.text: Court jurisdiction depends on the defendant's place of residence or the place where the offence was committed.

quiz.question: Which court is usually the first instance for simple cases?
What is a lay judge in court?
What determines court jurisdiction?
answers: The defendant's place of residence or the place of the offence

modules.title: Judicial Supervision
modules.text: The Supreme Court exercises supervision over the activities of common and military courts in the field of adjudication.

modules.title: Cassation Appeal
modules.text: A cassation appeal may be filed with the Supreme Court against a final judgment of the second instance.

modules.title: Chambers of the Supreme Court
modules.text: The Supreme Court is divided into specialized chambers (e.g. Criminal Chamber, Civil Chamber).

modules.title: Resolutions of the Supreme Court
modules.text: The Supreme Court adopts resolutions aimed at clarifying legal provisions that raise doubts.

modules.title: First President of the Supreme Court
modules.text: The work of the Supreme Court is directed by the First President, appointed by the President for a 6-year term.

modules.title: Seat
modules.text: The seat of the Supreme Court is located in Warsaw.

quiz.question: What is the name of the legal remedy directed to the Supreme Court?
How long is the term of office of the First President of the Supreme Court?
Where is the seat of the Supreme Court?

modules.title: Control of Administration
modules.text: Administrative courts control the legality of decisions issued by offices and public authorities.

modules.title: WSA
modules.text: Voivodeship Administrative Courts (WSA) hear complaints against administrative decisions in the first instance.

modules.title: NSA
modules.text: The Supreme Administrative Court (NSA) in Warsaw hears appeals against WSA judgments.

modules.title: Legality Criterion
modules.text: An administrative court examines only whether the authority acted in accordance with the law, it does not assess expediency.

modules.title: Annulment of a Decision
modules.text: In case of a breach of law, the administrative court annuls the challenged decision.

modules.title: Complaint for Inaction
modules.text: It is also possible to complain to the administrative court about an authority’s failure to respond within the deadline (inaction).

quiz.question: What do administrative courts deal with?
answers: Controlling the legality of administrative decisions

quiz.question: Where is the Supreme Administrative Court (NSA) located?
Does an administrative court assess the expediency of an authority’s actions?

modules.title: Role of the Supreme Court Chamber
modules.text: The Supreme Court is the competent body for declaring the validity of elections and referendums.

modules.title: Election Protests
modules.text: Citizens have the right to file protests with the Supreme Court regarding the validity of elections within a specified deadline.

modules.title: Analysis of Protests
modules.text: The Supreme Court examines whether the reported procedural violations affected the final election result.

modules.title: Resolution on Validity
modules.text: After examining the protests, the Supreme Court adopts a comprehensive resolution on the validity of parliamentary or presidential elections.

modules.title: EP Elections
modules.text: The Supreme Court also decides on the validity of elections to the European Parliament in Poland.

modules.title: Referendum
modules.text: The same procedure applies to nationwide referendums and confirming their legality.

quiz.question: Which institution declares the validity of parliamentary elections in Poland?
Who can file an election protest with the Supreme Court?
answers: Any voter entitled to participate in the elections

quiz.question: Can the Supreme Court invalidate elections?
answers: Yes, if procedural violations affected the result
No, the Supreme Court only declares validity
Yes, at the request of the President

modules.title: Court over the Law
modules.text: The Constitutional Tribunal (TK) examines the conformity of statutes with the Constitution of the Republic of Poland.

modules.title: Composition of the Tribunal
modules.text: The Constitutional Tribunal consists of 15 judges elected by the Sejm for a 9-year term.

modules.title: Constitutional Complaint
modules.text: Any citizen, after exhausting the judicial path, may file a constitutional complaint if they believe a provision has violated their rights.

modules.title: Legal Questions
modules.text: Courts may refer legal questions to the Constitutional Tribunal if they have doubts about the constitutionality of a provision.

modules.title: Finality of Judgments
modules.text: Rulings of the Constitutional Tribunal have universally binding force and are final.

modules.title: Derogation
modules.text: A provision declared unconstitutional by the Tribunal loses its legal force.

quiz.question: How many judges sit in the Constitutional Tribunal?
Can one appeal against a judgment of the Constitutional Tribunal?
Who can file a constitutional complaint with the Tribunal?
answers: Any citizen after exhausting the judicial path

modules.title: Criminal Liability of Officials
modules.text: The Tribunal of State tries the highest state officials for violations of the Constitution or statutes.

modules.title: Who is Accountable
modules.text: The Tribunal of State hears cases against, among others, the President, Prime Minister, government members, and the President of the Supreme Audit Office.

modules.title: Chairman
modules.text: The Chairman of the Tribunal of State is, by virtue of office, the current First President of the Supreme Court.

modules.title: Election of Members
modules.text: The remaining members of the Tribunal of State are elected by the Sejm for the term of its office from persons outside the group of MPs.

modules.title: Punishments
modules.text: The Tribunal of State may impose penalties such as loss of voting rights, prohibition on holding public positions, or deprivation of orders.

modules.title: Separate Nature
modules.text: The Tribunal of State is not a common court and convenes only to adjudicate specific indictments.

quiz.question: Who serves as Chairman of the Tribunal of State by virtue of office?
answers: President of the Republic of Poland
First President of the Supreme Court

quiz.question: What penalty can the Tribunal of State impose?
Is the Tribunal of State a common court?
answers: No, it is a separate body

modules.title: Guardian of Independence
modules.text: The National Council of the Judiciary (KRS) guards the independence of courts and the impartiality of judges.

modules.title: Judicial Appointments
modules.text: The KRS reviews candidacies for judges and presents proposals to the President for their appointment.

modules.title: Composition of the KRS
modules.text: The KRS consists of 25 members, including representatives of judges, MPs, senators, and the President.

modules.title: Minister in the KRS
modules.text: The Minister of Justice is also a member of the KRS by virtue of office.

modules.title: Term of Office
modules.text: The term of office of elected members of the National Council of the Judiciary is 4 years.

modules.title: Opinions
modules.text: The KRS issues opinions on draft legal acts concerning the judiciary and court system.

quiz.question: What is the main role of the National Council of the Judiciary (KRS)?
answers: Guarding the independence of courts and nominating judges

quiz.question: How many members does the National Council of the Judiciary have?
Who is a member of the KRS by virtue of office?
answers: President of the Republic of Poland

modules.title: Appointment
modules.text: Judges are appointed by the President of the Republic of Poland upon the motion of the National Council of the Judiciary.

modules.title: Irremovability
modules.text: Judges are irremovable – they cannot be dismissed except by a final decision of a disciplinary court.

modules.title: Judicial Immunity
modules.text: A judge cannot be held criminally liable or detained without the consent of a disciplinary court.

modules.title: Apolitical Nature of Judges
modules.text: A judge may not belong to a political party or engage in public activity incompatible with the judicial office.

modules.title: Protection from Pressure
modules.text: No one is allowed to influence a judge’s decisions or instruct them on how to adjudicate.

modules.title: Retirement
modules.text: Upon reaching a certain age, judges retire (judicial pension).

quiz.question: Who officially appoints judges in Poland?
answers: The President of the Republic of Poland

quiz.question: Can a judge in Poland belong to a political party?
What does judicial immunity mean?
answers: A judge cannot be held criminally liable without the consent of a disciplinary court
A judge is immune from punishment
A judge does not have to pay taxes

modules.title: Role of the Prosecution
modules.text: The Public Prosecution Service is responsible for prosecuting crimes and safeguarding the rule of law.

modules.title: Prosecutor General
modules.text: The head of the prosecution service is the Prosecutor General, who is currently the Minister of Justice by virtue of office.

modules.title: National Prosecutor
modules.text: The National Prosecutor is the direct head of the operational structures of the prosecution service and reports to the Prosecutor General.

modules.title: Indictment
modules.text: After collecting evidence by the Police, the prosecutor prepares and files an indictment with the court.

modules.title: Public Accuser
modules.text: In criminal proceedings, the prosecutor acts as the public accuser before the court.

modules.title: Independence of Investigation
modules.text: A prosecutor conducting an investigation has autonomy in making procedural decisions.

quiz.question: Who serves as Prosecutor General in Poland by virtue of office?
answers: The First President of the Supreme Court

quiz.question: What is the name of the document filed by the prosecutor to initiate criminal proceedings?
Does a prosecutor have the right to conduct investigations independently?
answers: Yes, they have autonomy in procedural decisions
No, they must consult with the Minister
Yes, but only in civil cases

modules.title: Right to a Court
modules.text: Everyone has the right to a fair and public hearing of their case without undue delay.

modules.title: Presumption of Innocence
modules.text: Everyone is presumed innocent until proven guilty by a final court judgment.

modules.title: Right to Defence
modules.text: The accused in criminal proceedings has the right to defence and the assistance of a lawyer.

modules.title: Two-Instance Proceedings
modules.text: A citizen who disagrees with a first-instance judgment has the right to file an appeal.

modules.title: Right to an Interpreter
modules.text: A person who does not speak Polish has the right to free interpreter assistance in court.

modules.title: Complaint about Delay
modules.text: If a case is taking too long, the citizen may file a complaint about the length of proceedings.

quiz.question: What does the presumption of innocence mean?
answers: The accused must prove they are innocent
The accused is considered innocent until a final judgment

quiz.question: What can a citizen do if they disagree with a first-instance judgment?
answers: File an appeal to a higher court
Write a complaint to the Prime Minister

quiz.question: Does a person who does not speak Polish have the right to an interpreter in court?
answers: Yes, they have the right to free interpreter assistance
Yes, but they must pay for it

modules.title: Judge
modules.text: A judge is an impartial arbiter who presides over hearings and issues judgments based on evidence.

modules.title: Advocate (Attorney)
modules.text: An advocate (defence counsel) represents clients, advises them, and defends the accused in criminal trials.

modules.title: Legal Advisor
modules.text: A legal advisor provides legal assistance, prepares legal opinions, and represents parties before civil and administrative courts.

modules.title: Legal Apprenticeship
modules.text: To practice these professions, after law studies one must complete several years of practical training – legal apprenticeship.

modules.title: Notary
modules.text: A notary is a public trust person authorized to draw up notarial deeds and certifications.

modules.title: Court Bailiff
modules.text: A court bailiff is a public official who enforces court judgments by compulsory means (e.g. debt collection).

quiz.question: Who represents the interests of the accused as their defence counsel in a criminal trial?
What is the name of the practical training after law studies preparing for professions such as judge or legal advisor?
What is the role of a notary in the legal system?
answers: Drawing up notarial deeds and certifications

questions.question: To whom are independent judges in Poland subordinate?
How many instances does court proceedings in Poland have?
What role does the Supreme Court play?
answers: It exercises jurisdictional supervision over common and military courts
It rules on the compliance of laws with the Constitution

questions.question: What is the Constitutional Tribunal?
answers: A court ruling on the compliance of law with the Constitution
A body for punishing senior officials

questions.question: What is the role of the Prosecutor General?
answers: Heads the prosecutor's office and supervises investigations

questions.question: What is cassation in court proceedings?
answers: An extraordinary remedy reviewed by the Supreme Court
An appeal to an appellate court

questions.question: Who represents the accused as their defense counsel in a criminal trial?
Who prepares notarial deeds and certifications?
What is the role of the Ombudsman (Commissioner for Human Rights)?
answers: Guards the freedoms and rights of persons and citizens

questions.question: What is the name of the practical training after law studies preparing for the legal profession?

SURVEY_NEW.title: New survey
SURVEY_NEW.message: You have a new survey to complete.

LAW_UPDATE.title: Law update
LAW_UPDATE.message: A new legal update is available.

notifications.description: Here you will find everything you participated in!
notifications.clearMessage: Are you sure you want to delete all read notifications?

documents.description: Important documents regarding the functioning of the website and its legal aspects.

contact.description: Let's get in touch! Do you have any questions, suggestions, or need assistance? Contact us and our team will be happy to help you. Fill out the contact form or use the contact details below to reach out to us. We look forward to hearing from you!
contact.name: Name

faq.description: Answer to the most frequently asked questions about using the mPanstwo platform. Find quick solutions and tips to make the most of our service. If you can't find an answer, contact us directly!

createAccount.question: How can I create an account?
createAccount.answer: Click the registration button and fill out the account creation form.

editProfile.question: Can I change my profile information?
editProfile.answer: Yes, you can edit all your information in the “My Profile” section.

deleteAccount.question: How can I delete my account?
deleteAccount.answer: The account deletion option is available in the security settings under the “My Profile” section.

resetPassword.question: How can I reset my password?
resetPassword.answer: Click “Forgot password” on the login screen and follow the instructions.

dataSecurity.question: Is my data secure?
dataSecurity.answer: Yes, user data is encrypted and properly secured.

accountHijacked.question: What should I do if someone gains access to my account?
accountHijacked.answer: Immediately change your password and contact the platform administration.

freeUsage.question: Is the website completely free to use?
freeUsage.answer: Yes, the website is intended for public use and is completely free.

contentModeration.question: Is the content on the website moderated?
contentModeration.answer: Yes, content is moderated by the platform administration to remove offensive and vulgar comments.

contactAdmin.question: How can I contact the administration?
contactAdmin.answer: You can use the contact form or email mpanstwosupport@gmail.com.

technicalProblem.question: How can I report a technical issue?
technicalProblem.answer: Report technical issues through the “Contact” section.

platformRules.question: Where can I find the platform rules?
platformRules.answer: The rules are available in the “Documents” section under “Platform Rules”.

reportViolation.question: How can I report a community guideline violation?
reportViolation.answer: Each comment has an option to report violations.

rulePenalties.question: What penalties apply to users who violate the rules?
rulePenalties.answer: All penalties are described in the platform regulations.

reset.resetNextStep: In the next step, you will provide your email address where we will send a password reset link.

profileAccount.verifiedEmailDesc: Your identity has been confirmed. Your account is secure.
profileAccount.notVerifiedEmailDesc: Confirm your address to unlock all system features.
profileAccount.notStrongPassword: Your password is too weak. We recommend using a combination of letters, numbers, and special characters.
profileAccount.changePasswordDesc: Changing your password regularly increases the security of your data.
profileAccount.changeEmailDesc: Your email is the key to accessing your account.
profileAccount.changeEmailCardDesc: The new email will be used for login and notifications.
profileAccount.changeEmailStepText: In the next step you will enter the new address and the password to confirm the change.
profileAccount.changeNameCardDesc: You will change the display name used throughout the system.
profileAccount.changeNameStepText: The name will be visible to other users.
profileAccount.changePasswordCardDesc: Make sure the new password is strong.
profileAccount.changePasswordStepText: After continuing you will enter the old and new password.
profileAccount.verifyEmailCardDesc: Click the button to send a code to your email.
profileAccount.verifyEmailStepText: After continuing you will receive a verification code by email that you will need to enter.
